english: a circle as an oblation, Mil. and elsewh.; tsogs-。kór (tshogs 'khor) skor-ba (skor ba) prob., like śóm-pa (shom pa) to prepare such an offering; tsogs ɣnyis (tshogs gnyis) Glr. was explained by bsod-nams-kyi tsogs (bsod nams kyi tshogs) daṅ (dang) ye-śes-kyi tsogs (ye shes kyi tshogs); sna-tsógs (sna tshogs) of all kinds, merely signifies 'many'. - 3. tsogs drug (tshogs drug) Mil. and elsewh., Was. 290, 'kinds' of perception by the senses, which are supposed to be more or less in number, yet the etymology of the word rather suggests the groups of objects perceptible by means of the (6) senses. - Comp. tsogs-káṅ (tshogs khang) meeting-house Cs. - tsogs-。kór (tshogs 'khor) v. above. - tsogs-grál (tshogs gral) Mil. 1. row of people in an assembly 2. row of offerings, ni f. - tsógs-càn-ma (tshogs can ma) Sch. 'songstress, prostitute'. - tsogs-mčóg (tshogs mchog) a most splendid assemblage, tsogs-mčog-dge-。dún (tshogs mchog dge 'dun) Thgy. - tsogs-gtám (tshogs gtam) speech addressed to a meeting Cs. - tsogs-stón (tshogs ston) a high sacrificial festival Pth. - tsogs(-kyi)-bdág(-po) (tshogs kyi bdag po) गणेश, son of Siwa, the god of wisdom, furnished with a thick belly and the head of an elephant; appears also in the Buddhism of later times. - tsogs-dpón (tshogs dpon) president or chairman of a meeting Cs. - tsogs-záṅs (tshogs zangs) Sch.: 'the meeting-kettle, the point of union or its symbol'. - tsogs-sa (tshogs sa) place of meeting Cs. - tsogs-gsóg (tshogs gsog) accumulated merit, tantamount to offerings and gifts bestowed on priests, also any service or work done to or for a priest Mil.

(Cs. = zoṅ (zong) merchandize, but more corr.:) trade, traffic, commerce, *pag-tsoṅ* (*phag tshong*) W. smuggling-trade, *čó-ce* (*cho ce*), *táṅ-če* (*tang che*); tsoṅ-gi k̇e (tshong gi khe) profit, gain, gun (gun) loss in trading; tsoṅ byéd-pa (tshong byed pa) Glr., *gyag-pa* (*rgyag pa*) C., *gyab-če* (*rgyab ce*) W. (cf. above), to carry on trade; tsoṅ brgýud-pa (tshong brgyud pa) id. Sch. Comp. tsoṅ-skad (tshong skad) commercial language, business-like style, terms of trade. - tsoṅ-kaṅ (tshong khang) store-house, magazine. - tsoṅ-gru (tshong gru) trading-vessel, merchantman. - tsoṅ-grogs (tshong grogs) commercial friend, correspondent. - tsoṅ-čaṅ (tshong chang) pledging in beer, after a bargain has been struck. - tsoṅ-čad (tshong chad) bill of purchase, deed of sale. - tsoṅ-mtun (tshong mthun) commercial intercourse. - tsoṅ-。dus (tshong 'dus) market people Pth. - tsoṅ-。dus-sa (tshong 'dus sa) market-place. - tsoṅ-rdal (tshong rdal) that quarter of a city which is chiefly inhabited by merchants. - tsoṅ-pa (tshong pa) merchant, trader, seller; 。bru-tsoṅ-pa ('bru tshong pa) corn-merchant, čaṅ-tsoṅ-pa (chang tshong pa) dealer in wine and other liquors. - gser-daṅ-dṅul-(gyi) tsóṅ-pa (gser dang dngul gyi tshong pa) exchanger of gold and silver coins. - tsoṅ-dpon (tshong dpon), Hind. chaudhari, head of a commercial establishment, the principal merchant in a city, under whose control all the rest, and the market in general, are standing; the chief leader of a caravan, to whom all that have joined in it are subordinate Glr. - tsoṅ-spógs (tshong spogs) proceeds of trade; tsoṅ-spógs byéd-pa (tshong spogs byed pa), tsoṅ-spógs-la 。gró-ba (tshong spogs la 'gro ba) to engage in commercial speculations Dzl. - tsoṅ-。prúl (tshong 'phrul) commerce, tsoṅ-。prúl-gyi gnas (tshong 'phrul gyi gnas) market. - tsoṅ-zán (tshong zan) (cf. tsoṅ-čaṅ (tshong chang)) meal after settling a business. - tsoṅ-zóṅ (tshong zong) goods, merchandize. - tsóṅ-sa (tshong sa) commercial place, market.
1. a kind of ornament Cs. 2. = tsoṅ-tsón (tshong tshong).

(prop. the same as tsad (tshad)) 1. measure, proportion, in a general sense = the right and just measure; tsod 。dzin-pa (tshod 'dzin pa), (bzúṅ-ba) (bzung ba) W. *zúm-če* (*zum che*) 1. to take measure, to measure, to measure out, to survey, yul (yul) land, *yul-tsód-zum-k̇an* (*yul tshod zum mkhan*) land-surveyor W. 2. to estimate, to rate, to appraise, to tax, raṅ-gi tsod mi 。dzin (rang gi tshod mi 'dzin) he overrates himself (his own powers) Dzl. 3. to observe the right measure, to be temperate, zas-čáṅ-la (zas chang la) in eating and drinking Glr.; zás-tsod ma zin čuṅs gyúr-na (zas tshod ma zin chungs gyur na) when below the proper measure, i.e. when too little is eaten S.g. 4. to try, to tempt, to lead into temptation W.; tsod-ltá-ba (tshod lta ba), lén-pa (len pa) B. and vulg., Cs. also tsod bgám-pa (tshod bgam pa) to try, prove, *tsod ma ltos* (*tshod ma ltos*) I have not tried it yet W., *tig-tsód ma ltos* (*thig tshod ma ltos*) id., tsod ltá-ba (tshod lta ba), len-pa (len pa) also to sound, to sift, examine, spy out, tsód-len-pa (tshod len pa) sbst., spy; séms-kyi (sems kyi) or nyáms-(kyi) tsod lén-pa (nyams kyi tshod len pa) to examine, find out or sift another's thoughts or sentiments, also *k̇og-tsó' lém-pa* (*khog tshod len pa*) C.; tsod jal-ba (tshod 'jal ba) to measure; tsod-śes-pa (tshod shes pa) to keep measure, and adj.: observing due measure, temperate, tsod-mi-śés-pa (tshod mi shes pa) not keeping measure, intemperate. - tsod-càn (tshod can), tsod-ldan (tshod ldan) 1. moderate. 2. punctilious, strict, grave W. - tsod-méd (tshod med) intemperate, immoderate, im-
